country.name: Republic of Veloria
country.politicalSystem: Presidential republic under rolling emergency powers
country.description: A coastal transit state where gas wealth, patronage networks, and a restless conscript army pull politics in opposite directions.
country.tagline: Ports, pipelines, and a brittle executive center.

government.ideology: order-first siege statism wrapped in patriotic necessity
government.narrativeStyle: executive certainty, paternal nationalism, clipped technocratic confidence
government.longGame: Keep the capital and the officer corps aligned long enough to outlast unrest driven by Regional inequality.
government.contradiction: Needs legitimacy but keeps relying on instruments that deepen anger around Youth unemployment.

opposition.ideology: anti-corruption civic populism with coalition instincts
opposition.narrativeStyle: moral indictment, constitutional language, wounded civic pride
opposition.longGame: Reframe Regional inequality as proof that the current order has broken the social contract.
opposition.contradiction: Needs mass pressure and elite reassurance at the same time, especially while Youth unemployment remains unresolved.

lobby_groups.ideology: transnational rent protection with local political brokerage
lobby_groups.narrativeStyle: transactional realism, boardroom understatement, veiled threats
lobby_groups.longGame: Keep Offshore gas profitable regardless of who occupies the ministries.
lobby_groups.contradiction: Fears collapse but often intensifies it when protecting margins under Rare-earth logistics.

citizens.ideology: municipal mutualism that wants safety without surrendering dignity
citizens.narrativeStyle: plainspoken anger, survival logic, collective dignity
citizens.longGame: Force elites to treat Regional inequality as a lived emergency rather than a slogan.
citizens.contradiction: People want order and revolt at once when daily life keeps deteriorating around Youth unemployment.

redLines: covering a crisis nobody can explain
foreign_media.narrativeStyle: compressed moral framing mixed with geopolitical shorthand
foreign_media.longGame: Turn instability around Offshore gas and Regional inequality into a legible international storyline.
foreign_media.contradiction: Needs spectacle for attention but loses interest when crises become structurally complex instead of cinematic.

actions.rationale: The presidency leans on patriotic messaging to project control while avoiding a more expensive intervention. Defensive and suspicious, the faction frames this move through order-first siege statism wrapped in patriotic necessity.

Organizers push for a protest surge in Red Basin to build a visible center of gravity. Organizing for a broader opening, the faction frames this move through anti-corruption civic populism with coalition instincts.

Lobby actors exploit volatility while keeping a back door open to whoever controls the ministries next. Quietly consolidating influence, the faction frames this move through transnational rent protection with local political brokerage.

Residents in Red Basin build local coping structures because national institutions feel unreliable. Restless and watchful, the faction frames this move through municipal mutualism that wants safety without surrendering dignity.

Outside coverage decides whether the crisis is a democratic opening, a market warning, or just background noise. Searching for a decisive frame, the faction frames this move through rights-first international crisis framing.

events.headline: The turn closes in fragile calm
events.detail: Street energy and elite maneuvering remain active, but no single flashpoint dominates beyond Civic Ring.

summary.headline: Republic of Veloria enters turn 1 under renewed political strain
summary.summary: Government and Opposition set the tone this turn while street pressure and elite maneuvering reshaped the balance. The turn closes in fragile calm

events.headline: Opening briefing for Republic of Veloria
events.detail: Port Azure faces an unstable opening balance between elite control, public frustration, and outside scrutiny.

summary.headline: Republic of Veloria starts in a fragile equilibrium
summary.summary: Republic of Veloria opens with layered political stress around Port Azure. The dashboard is ready to simulate how government, opposition, lobbies, citizens, and foreign media push the country toward survival or rupture.
reasons: Baseline stability begins under pressure from regional inequality.
Foreign influence is already meaningful around strategic resources and media attention.
Military loyalty and public trust will determine whether tension becomes transition or coercion.
